document.source_title: Assistant Secretary of War for Health Affairs
document.summary: Keith Bass serves as the Assistant Secretary of War for Health Affairs, advising on health and force health protection policies for the Military Health System, which serves 9.5 million beneficiaries. Previously, he directed the West Texas VA Health Care System, managing services for over 24,000 veterans. His experience includes leading the White House Medical Unit and serving as the first non-physician Director of the CIA's Office of Medical Services. Bass holds multiple degrees in psychology, rehabilitation science, and health care administration.
key_takeaways: Principal advisor to the Secretary of War on health policies.
Leads a health care network serving 9.5 million beneficiaries.
Managed health care for over 24,000 veterans at West Texas VA.
First non-physician Director of the CIA's Office of Medical Services.
Experience in telehealth and virtual patient care.
Retired U.S. Navy commander with multiple professional affiliations.
document.why_it_matters: Keith Bass's role is crucial in shaping health policies for military personnel and their families, impacting the overall health care delivery system within the Department of Defense and beyond.
